title: Board Foot Calculator - Lumber BF Measurement
shortDescription: Calculate board feet for lumber from thickness, width, length, and quantity.
description: Quickly calculate board feet (BF) for any lumber or woodworking project. Enter thickness in inches, width in inches, length in feet, and the number of pieces to get the total board footage instantly.
metaDescription: Free board foot calculator for lumber and woodworking. Enter thickness, width, length, and quantity to get total board feet (BF) instantly and accurately.

result.label: Board feet
result.empty: Enter thickness, width, length, and quantity to see board feet.

sections.heading: What is a board foot?
paragraphs: A board foot is a unit of volume used to measure rough lumber. One board foot equals 144 cubic inches, the volume of a board 1 inch thick, 12 inches wide, and 12 inches long.
Lumber yards price hardwood by the board foot, so knowing how to calculate it helps you compare prices and order the right amount.

sections.heading: The board foot formula
paragraphs: Board feet = (thickness in inches × width in inches × length in feet ÷ 12) × quantity. The division by 12 converts the mixed inch-and-foot dimensions into board feet.
For example, a 2 in × 6 in × 8 ft board equals (2 × 6 × 8 ÷ 12) = 8 BF per piece.

howTo.name: Enter the thickness
howTo.text: Type the lumber thickness in inches, such as 1 or 2.

howTo.name: Enter width and length
howTo.text: Add the width in inches and the length in feet for one piece.

howTo.name: Set the quantity
howTo.text: Enter how many pieces you have to get the total board feet.

faq.a: BF stands for board feet, a volume unit equal to 144 cubic inches of lumber.
Because length is in feet while thickness and width are in inches, dividing by 12 converts the result to board feet.

faq.q: Does board foot measure actual size?
faq.a: It uses nominal dimensions for rough lumber. Surfaced boards may be slightly smaller than the nominal size.

faq.q: How do I price lumber by board foot?
faq.a: Multiply the total board feet by the price per board foot quoted by your supplier.

faq.q: Can I calculate multiple boards at once?
faq.a: Yes. Enter the quantity field with the number of identical pieces to get the combined board footage.
